summary: The dataset contains 1972 records with a mean quantity of 1148.9 units and a mean FOB value of $449,225. The data is skewed towards higher values, with a maximum quantity of 76,000 units and a maximum FOB value of $28,752,300.

tables.title: Data Preview
rows: Cumin Seeds-OTHER DETAILS AS PER INV & P

insights: The majority of the data is concentrated in the RITC Code 9093129, which corresponds to Cumin Seeds.
The top 3 countries of destination are IRAN, UZBEKISTAN, and other countries.
The average item rate is $4,520.07, with a standard deviation of $18,168.47.
The FOB value is highly variable, with a standard deviation of $1,820,508.
Further analysis is required to understand the market trends and demand for Cumin Seeds.
The high variability in FOB values suggests that there may be opportunities for cost optimization.
The data may be useful for identifying potential export markets and negotiating better prices with buyers.
suggested_questions: What is the distribution of FOB values across different RITC Codes, and which codes have the highest average FOB values?
How does the average item rate vary across different countries of destination?
What is the relationship between the quantity of goods and the FOB value, and are there any outliers in this relationship?
Can we identify any patterns or correlations between the SB.Date and the FOB value or quantity of goods?
How does the average FOB value and quantity of goods vary across different exporters, and are there any notable differences between them?
